reviews.review_text:
Rising Storm 2: Vietnam is still the gold standard for semi-casual milsim shooters in my eyes. It perfectly balances realism and accessibility. The gunplay feels deadly and immersive, the shooting mechanics are satisfying, and every engagement is intense without turning into a walking simulator. Unlike many hardcore milsims, you actually spend your time fighting instead of running for 15 minutes.

What really makes the game special though is the atmosphere. The sound design, the voice lines, the chaos of the battles and some gritty moments. Few games manage to create this kind of tension and immersion while still being genuinely fun to play casually.

Even years later, nothing quite scratches the same itch. If a proper remake or successor came out tomorrow, I’d buy it day one without hesitation.
Proof that gameplay beats graphics. This is everything HLL Vietnam wishes it could be.

When I was home after my first tour, it was worse. I'd wake up and there'd be nothing. I hardly said a word to my wife until I said yes to a divorce. When I was here, I wanted to be there. When I was there... all I could think of was getting back into the jungle.

Considering how old this game is, the Vietnam vibes are still way more on point and immersive in this game than any other new Vietnam games. Notably Hell Let Loose: Vietnam playtest was a massive let down and Im just gonna install RS2 again by this point. I just hope you're gonna make RS3 someday. I'd donate my left nutsack and my first born child for a RS3 game in my lifetime
great game, realistic, not very beginner friendly

VOIP being broken, aswell as publisher effectively abandoning the game is real shame. It's been years and only now getting signs of potential stability updates. Still, there's few games quite like RS2 regarding game loop, especially in Vietnam War era. Thumbs up, especially if on sale.

Really good game! 
Just expect in-game chat to get really political really fast and you'll be fine honestly, same as Red Orchestra 2 but a lot more arcade-y, like a LOT more arcade-like

It's actually kind of impressive the step down they took for this one, I haven't seen anything of '83 yet but I can only imagine they went further with this if nobody is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ with me about the time playing it- Red Orchestra 2 has tanks, this has helicopters, bit of a tradeoff but it's not too bad and has plenty of mechanics to pair with it anyway so you're chill

It's all good, some achievements are locked behind the green army men DLC which I haven't even touched yet, the base game is just that good of a game? There's a little P2W aspect, hardly though, you can purchase camouflage as the Viet Cong which arguably makes you harder to spot, you can unlock this after a few dozen hours anyway though so it's not all bad. 

Just get on and get into using local chat, it's a pretty quiet game today but I've genuinely met one of my best friends by roleplaying on local chat while everyone else ignored or chastised what I was doing. I know what I'm doing, I'm making friends :D

But yeah no if you've got a soft heart this isn't it for you either, dying sucks and you really get to hear it. Maybe not Verdun levels of death, Verdun does dying justice, but it can be uncomfortable. Though fwiw if you're here and reading this you're beyond ♥♥♥♥♥♥ and gonna buy anyway just for the death rattles, so good on you. 

tl:dr don't play anything else, just get this one from Tripwire. RO2/RS1 is also good but not as many people are on them either- The gameplay is also a lot more brutal in the previous entry. So maybe go there if that's your thing, the deaths are "better" there too.

RIP the British Tim, accidentally flamed four teammates in a row and got banned from the community server near instantly
I applaud your efforts over the local chat my friend, good show, good show indeed

The game is fun, Most of the negative reviews are from people with 2-5 hours in the game, complaining that they get shot out of no where because they don't understand you cant sprint in a open field at the obj without dying. (I'm the one shooting you from the obj you're sprint at, I see the level 0 next to your name in the kill feed) literally just follow your squad around, no one is gonna be good at any game within the first few matches they play. On top of that dying is part of them game, you will die trying to take the obj, that's just how it works, you're not Rambo. Or they can't find a game because they don't know how to open the server browser. It takes me literally 1 min to find a game, and a few more for a space in the server to open up for me.

Out of all the things you can leave a negative review for in this game, those two things aren't main factors just sort by player count on the server browser and don't sprint around without a head.

cheaters and admins with small man syndrome do happen, crappy admins are rare, and cheaters (at least in most of my games) get kicked by there team by the first match after people realize.

Game was great at first. then EG7 purchased the devteams tripwire and anitmatter games thus acquiring the risingstorm ip. Since then the updates have been few and far in between, the game is pretty much dead. would never recommend anything associated with Enad Global 7.
Highly recommend this game, Still seeing on average at least 5/6 servers full with 64 players and its still good fun to play.

Want to immerse yourself into a milsim vietnam game where you don't have to run half an hour to get to an objective? This is the one.

over almost 10 years old and immensely better than HLL Vietnam.
Timeless. No matter how many years pass, I always come back to this game. It's incredibly fun and I wish we could populate it more.
Game is great, it has more of a faster paced gameplay than RO2 and some of the maps are borrowed plus more active players. Overall game is amazing
I heard some Australian Marines coming up the stairs on me, I tossed a grenade down. I as I came down nothing but a torso and bits! INSTANT CLASSIC for me! Playing this until HLLV

Fun game, not a milsim, don't expect that and you will have fun.

It's more like insurgency if I had to compare it to something
best in the genre, the perfect blend between classic running simulator milsim and arcadey pick-up-and-play able battlefield type game

Excellent depiction of 'Nam.

It has good gun physics. There is artillery, air support, helicopters and even booby traps. Pretty much every typical Vietnam era weapon is there.

Game is hard, unforgiving, requires tactics and patience, but what would you expect from Vietnam afterall...

Worth checking out.
I like the game a lot. It feels good to have a fast paced shooter with an adequately realistic response to damage.

Why are the trees speaking Vietnamese? 
Anyway solid game sad they stop updating it 
But still good mods
Frankly one of the best tactical action fps games out there. Fast paced, skill is rewarded, and since beta testing its been fresh and fun.
Amazing game. Worth it on sales and pretty good playerbase. However, servers are mainly based in US and EU
Never played a shooter game as thought out as this every aspect of either teams equipment and what they can do is entirely different yet somehow balanced

dude, you want vietnam, you got it. if your good at arma, then this is a cake walk, if your good at cod, think of hardcore mode, in bo1, never played the new ones past 2019 so cant say. This is beautiful on my ROG pc, actually stunning, im from the xbox with this being one of the first games i bought on pc, 6 full in the bush hours and im in love with both sides, heli is super fun and easy to transport, or super annoying killing your teamates learning. buy this, love this, cherish it.

honestly this is one game i always find myself coming back to even though there are games like squad, hell let loose and many more nothing feels quite like rising storm and in my opinion it still holds up and not to mention they just updated the game for the first time in awhile today so i hope more people flood the servers i would love to experience prime rising storm one more time <3
the soundtrack is peak, the community is peak, AND the gameplay is peak. what more could you ask for.
One of the best multiplayer fps games I have ever played. Low player count so get it on sale.

This is a MUST PLAY in my opinion.
It is an older game and the servers are still somewhat active.
Even playing on a server with only a few players is still fun and the maps still work well with a small number of players. So keep that in mind when joining a server.

The first definitive Vietnam War game experience. Excellent gameplay with immersive weapons and map variety

10/10 Will Run Through the Jungle again and again ;)

First off games a bit dated and clunky with movement and mantling, but if you can get past that its a pretty solid game with great sound design and weapon feel. I say get it on SALE and don't get dissuaded from dying a lot and wondering where you've been shot from, its a Vietnam game after all and I love that !
Bought the game, joined the server and dont know anything, few seconds my team blows up and i get shot out of nowhere. even though its kinda painful but i love the game
Great game, sad to see the loss of support.

♥♥♥♥ '83, ♥♥♥♥ Hell Let Loose Vietnam. This is all you need. I'm looking at 7 or 8 full servers and It's been 9 years. Lightning never strikes in the same place twice and that place is Vietnam.

It's a neat game although it's a strictly multiplayer, tactical shooter, with a short time to kill and filled with spammables like rpgs, grenade launchers, recon aircraft, napalm, etc. that ruin any kind of balance. Add to that a pretty long respawn time.
The best Vietnam experience, even by June 18th 2026, nearly 10 years later.
